channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-18135] sanity-lnet test_250: test_250 failed with 1: Error: argument "eth0" is wrong: table id value is invalid
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for Chris Horn <chris.horn@hpe.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/cd605fdb-db6b-4524-8e1a-af736c9adb13

test_250 failed with the following error:

test_250 failed with 1

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/106749 - 4.18.0-513.24.1.el8_9.x86_64
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/106749 - 4.18.0-513.24.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

The test suite was running under IPv6 configuration

[14768.391232] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: == sanity-lnet test 250: test that linux routes are added ========================================================== 01:42:49 (1723254169)
[14768.403873] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lnetctl lnet unconfigure
[14768.408152] LNet: 1183401:0:(lib-ptl.c:966:lnet_clear_lazy_portal()) Active lazy portal 0 on exit
[14768.417167] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lnetctl lnet configure -l
[14768.428658] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lnetctl net add --net tcp --if eth0
[14768.434301] LNet: Added LNI fd33:3981:3213:f020:0:5254:9:7574@tcp [8/256/0/180]
[14768.436281] LNet: Accept all, port 7988
[14768.684449] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lctl mark  sanity-lnet test_250: @@@@@@ FAIL: test_250 failed with 1 

== sanity-lnet test 250: test that linux routes are added ========================================================== 01:42:49 (1723254169)
/usr/sbin/lnetctl lnet unconfigure
/usr/sbin/lnetctl lnet configure -l
/usr/sbin/lnetctl net add --net tcp --if eth0
Error: argument "eth0" is wrong: table id value is invalid

 sanity-lnet test_250: @@@@@@ FAIL: test_250 failed with 1

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
sanity-lnet test_250 - test_250 failed with 1
item.summary: sanity-lnet test_250: test_250 failed with 1: Error: argument "eth0" is wrong: table id value is invalid

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: Thank you Cyril. Can you resolve the ticket then.
Today we got a failure, but same problem: patch was not rebased recently.

Let me clarify, it seems I was not clear…
The failures in autotest for master branch were from patches that were not rebased and did not contain "LU-18135 scripts: call ksocklnd-config with IPv6" fix.
So, everything is fine.
Just searched on Maloo and found sanity-lnet test 250 consistently passed on master branch after 2025-12-09.
Which patch is missing for master?
Hello yujian. I checked the failures in the last 2 weeks on master and none had the fix. That explains the failures…
74 records found in the last 2 weeks on master branch.

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/62414/
Subject: LU-18135 scripts: bad iproute rt_tables path
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: b2_15
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 59946ea7441e1792098e0b88607f320e22f8588e

"Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/62414
Subject: LU-18135 scripts: bad iproute rt_tables path
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: b2_15
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 2ceff16e22bc4b1f8c1161810520263d97334529

The patch just landed, so will have to check in a week after patches have been rebased to see if the review-dne-ipv6 test sessions are now passing this subtest on master:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/search?client_branch=master&horizon=172800&ipv_filter=ipv6&test_set_script_id=a2b1c4b2-b449-11e9-b88c-52540065bddc&sub_test_script_id=00a08994-76b0-4e8c-8889-7e7ddbfff030&source=sub_tests#redirect

So far all 27/27 test_250 runs on master in the past 3 days have still failed, but 4/4 on master-next have passed.

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61649/
Subject: LU-18135 scripts: call ksocklnd-config with IPv6
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: adf0af8ba28b0ce9e1996fc468119ce932b91d6c

It looks like this is the last regular IPv6 subtest failure in the review-dne-ipv6 sessions, so once this is landed (hopefully today) and those sessions are passing regularly we can look at changing them to be enforced.

"Cyril Bordage <cbordage@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61649
Subject: LU-18135 scripts: call ksocklnd-config with IPv6
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 70efb775838c3f4ef9a6cc8597f39fcfd07a48d0

Failed on master branch with IPv6:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/78dc0768-5f14-4dfc-bf72-2314eee77756

From what I found, it works with IPv4. I checked again for the last few days, and it fails only with IPv6, or with IPv4 without the previous fix. Patches failing with IPv4 have to be rebased.
As it is very cumbersome to check every patch, I did check only ~50 patches, so I may have missed some failures, but the probability seems quite low.
I was not able to get a test env with IPv6 even after a commit with ~40 lines "Test-Parameters: testlist=sanity-lnet". Is there a good way for that? On my local machine, I was not able to get the same error as reported by autotest.

cbordage, while it may be true that this subtest fails with IPv6 (100% failure rate for the review-dne-ipv6 test session, AFAICS), it is definitely also failing with IPv4 since we have only started running IPv6 in the past week or so, and this subtest has been failing for many months. Please focus on getting IPv4 to pass consistently before working on IPv6 issues.
It works with IPv4 but fails with IPv6.
I checked a test failure and it got the patch. Will work on that.

Still seeing regular failures of this subtest on master (73 in the past week):
https://testing.whamcloud.com/search?client_branch=master&status%5B%5D=FAIL&test_set_script_id=a2b1c4b2-b449-11e9-b88c-52540065bddc&sub_test_script_id=00a08994-76b0-4e8c-8889-7e7ddbfff030&start_date=2025-08-21&end_date=2025-08-27&source=sub_tests#redirect

It is possible that some of these failures are based on old parents that do not contain patch 60250, but it seems unlikely that all of the tests being run have not been updated, given that the failure frequency has not gone down?

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/60250/
Subject: LU-18135 scripts: bad iproute rt_tables path
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: ff5042c83b82056745d472cc495f70c82c1189cf

"Cyril Bordage <cbordage@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/60250
Subject: LU-18135 scripts: bad iproute rt_tables path
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: fc65fe4a13dddce9346ba2129ce76171b7ebf649
This failed 94 times in the past week, across all main branches.

This subtest failed 52x on master in the past week, all of them with el9.6 clients, which we want to start enforcing test runs on:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/search?client_branch=master&status%5B%5D=FAIL&test_set_script_id=a2b1c4b2-b449-11e9-b88c-52540065bddc&sub_test_script_id=00a08994-76b0-4e8c-8889-7e7ddbfff030&start_date=2025-06-29&end_date=2025-07-05&source=sub_tests#redirect

Changing the search to show all test results for el9.6 shows that all test runs for el9.6 are failing with:

/usr/sbin/ksocklnd-config: line 123: /etc/iproute2/rt_tables: No such file or directory
/usr/sbin/ksocklnd-config: line 130: /etc/iproute2/rt_tables: No such file or directory

so it looks like a test/distro issue that needs to be fixed.
https://testing.whamcloud.com/search?client_branch=master&client_distribution=RHEL+9.6&test_set_script_id=a2b1c4b2-b449-11e9-b88c-52540065bddc&sub_test_script_id=00a08994-76b0-4e8c-8889-7e7ddbfff030&start_date=2025-07-01&end_date=2025-07-05&source=sub_tests#redirect

sanity-lnet test 250 failed as follows on RHEL 9.6 client:

== sanity-lnet test 250: test that linux routes are added ========================================================== 20:49:03 (1748292543)
Loading LNet and configuring DLC
Loading modules from /usr/lib64/lustre
detected 2 online CPUs by sysfs
MODOPTS_LIBCFS=
Force libcfs to create 2 CPU partitions
../lnet/lnet/lnet options: 'accept=all'
/usr/sbin/lnetctl lnet configure
/usr/sbin/lnetctl net add --net tcp --if eth0
/usr/sbin/ksocklnd-config: line 241: /etc/iproute2/rt_tables: No such file or directory
/usr/sbin/ksocklnd-config: line 248: /etc/iproute2/rt_tables: No such file or directory
Error: argument "eth0" is wrong: table id value is invalid

 sanity-lnet test_250: @@@@@@ FAIL: test_250 failed with 1

https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/1a8fe8f0-fe0e-4431-82b8-d8944edc97a5

"Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/59441
Subject: LU-18135 tests: run sanity-lnet/250 on RHEL 9.6 client
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 42353e4e2549fe458d6635bfa96bc5619631ea1a

I believe this is due to:

ip route show table ${INTERFACES[0]} | grep -q "${INTERFACES[0]}"

which is IPv4 specific.
